City Engineer Ralston was lam
basted on all sides at the meeting
of the city council last night He
was not present to hear the excor-
iation, but has been requested to
come to the next meeting to an
swer some questions as to his Off
cial conduct in the Washington
street bridge salvage, of which
$4,000 is reported to have disap-
peared.


Councilman Funk had a strong
resolution of censure for Ralston
because of the collapse of tie
Sprague avenue fill, for which RsI-
ston was held responsible. Funk
said that Ralston had consentHd
that the contractor should climb
ate the concrete base of the fill 1t
saving to the contractor of SIN
900, but for an allowance 80 tie
city in the price of the work Af
only $500. The restitution wis
modified to protect the city in tie
probable lawsuit because of tie
fill.


Councilman Mohr brought up Ct b
question of the Monroe strd5t
bridge and said that Ralston HI


erected large building at TIE
bridge, had asked for bids on Sh
million feet of lumber, was hrep&f-
ing to remove the lights from the
old bridge and about to begin to
tear it down, all before the council
had even approved the plan for the
new bridge or determined how the
bridge should be built, whether by
contract or by day labor.


A resolution by Mohr was adopt
ed ordering the engineer to leave
the old bridge intact until further
orders of the city council and that
no further. steps toward the build
ing of the bridge be taken beyond
carrying the piers and abutmerixs
above the high Water line, untfl
the council APPROVES the plans.
